pi_items.source: Wikipedia (Pound sterling) and official British currency documentation
Wikipedia (Date and time notation in the United Kingdom) and regional format standards
Wikipedia (Value-added tax in the United Kingdom) - standard tax system in UK at 20% standard rate
Found referenced in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts, but no authoritative public documentation available

privileged_info_summary: The task configures Copper Point of Sale (a retail POS system by NCH Software) for UK regional settings. The expected currency symbol is £ (British pound), which is correct for the United Kingdom. The expected date format is dd/MM/yyyy, which is the standard UK date format (Day/Month/Year). The expected tax name is VAT (Value Added Tax), which is the correct sales tax system in the United Kingdom with a standard rate of 20%. The registry path HKCU:\Software\NCH Software\Copper is referenced in the task scripts but could not be independently verified against public documentation. The task operates on synthetic test data (customers.csv and products.csv contain synthetic customer and product data).
